refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: terrain analysis, obstacle analysis, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs focusing solely on what the system must do without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis of the terrain characteristics of the worksite area independently from other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area
Extract and classify key site features relevant to planning and safety
Integrate terrain and feature data into a unified worksite map
Provide an interface for planning and execution functions to access the worksite model
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a terrain model independently, which can then be used by other DPs for feature extraction and integration, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of sufficient data to generate an accurate terrain model
Risk that terrain model alone may not capture all necessary site features without additional processing
Potential integration challenges if feature extraction and mapping are handled separately
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing the DP further to explicitly include feature extraction and integration steps to ensure completeness and modularity of the worksite analysis function.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core processing and data management functions, including terrain modeling and digital map generation for the worksite area.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and positional data from the Vehicle and other sources to build the terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and parameters related to terrain model generation and updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends the generated digital terrain model data to other modules or external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as terrain modeling is a core data processing function; minimal diffusion since data inputs and outputs are well defined.
Low containment as Vehicle focuses on physical operation; terrain modeling would require extensive data sharing and cause high diffusion.
Teleoperation is focused on remote control and monitoring, not data modeling; would cause high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, unrelated to terrain modeling; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation DP because it centralizes data processing and terrain modeling functions, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from Vehicle module.
Network Link interface must support sufficient bandwidth and reliability for terrain data transmission.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing the worksite area' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite area terrain' by specifying a concrete method to analyze the terrain through digital modeling, aligning well with the acceptance criteria for generating a worksite model/map.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (1). The module boundary encompasses core processing and data management functions including terrain modeling, minimizing diffusion risk. External interfaces are well defined and appropriate for data input and output, supporting good architectural packaging.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module outputs the digital terrain model via the Network Link interface, enabling validation against acceptance criteria that the model is available to planning/execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate is clearly the NGHE Solution module, which centralizes terrain modeling responsibilities, ensuring clear allocation of the FR to a single implementation module.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and mitigate dependency risks on timely and accurate telemetry data from the Vehicle module to ensure terrain model accuracy.
Ensure the Network Link interface supports sufficient bandwidth and reliability for transmitting the digital terrain model data to downstream modules.
Consider documenting explicit verification steps to confirm the generated terrain model meets the agreed set of site features and is accessible to planning/execution functions.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and no significant diffusion risks or verification issues. The DP directly satisfies the FR, and the IM allocation is clear and well-contained. Recommended revisions are monitoring and documentation actions rather than structural changes. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further sub-FR derivation at this time.

revise.why: No structural revision needed; only monitoring and verification documentation recommended to mitigate dependency and interface risks.

assumptions: Telemetry data from Vehicle module will be timely and accurate.
Network Link interface will maintain required bandwidth and reliability.
open_risks: Dependency on telemetry data accuracy and timeliness.
Potential network interface limitations affecting data transmission.
evidence_needed: Verification steps confirming the digital terrain model meets site feature criteria.
Validation of data transmission reliability over Network Link interface.
